texto: In Venice, things not always as they first appear. I contemplate this observation from my post on the aft deck of one of Master Fumagalli’s gondolas, taking in the panorama of bridges, domes, bell towers, and quaysides of my native city. I row into the neck of the Grand Canal, and, one by one, the reflection of each colorful façade appears, only to dissipate into wavering, shimmering shards under my oar.
explicacao: Citação de Laura Morelli sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “In Venice, things not always as they first appear. I contemplate this observation from my post on…” articula reflexao, coming-of-age, gondola em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como gondola e historical-fiction.
